name: International Drive
description: Orlando's primary tourist corridor featuring ICON Park, convention center, hundreds of hotels, restaurants, and attractions along a 15-mile stretch.

name: Downtown Orlando
description: The urban core of Orlando featuring Lake Eola, the Amway Center, Wall Street Plaza, and a growing residential district with nightlife and dining.

name: Lake Nona
description: Orlando's master-planned medical city and tech hub southeast of downtown, home to major hospitals, the USTA campus, and innovative wellness community.

name: Dr. Phillips
description: Upscale Orlando neighborhood known as Restaurant Row, featuring hundreds of dining options along Sand Lake Road and proximity to theme parks.
highlights: Dr. Phillips Center for the Performing Arts

name: Celebration
description: Master-planned community originally developed by The Walt Disney Company, featuring charming downtown area, lakefront, and proximity to Disney World.

name: Lake Buena Vista
description: The municipality containing Walt Disney World Resort, Disney Springs, and dozens of official and partner hotels serving millions of visitors annually.

name: Winter Park
description: Charming affluent suburb north of Orlando featuring Park Avenue boutiques, Rollins College, scenic boat tours, and world-class dining.

name: Universal Boulevard Area
description: The corridor surrounding Universal Orlando Resort, featuring major hotels, the convention center south end, and easy access to I-4.

name: US-192 Corridor
description: Kissimmee's main tourist strip along Irlo Bronson Memorial Highway, featuring budget hotels, vacation rentals, restaurants, and attractions near Disney.

name: Downtown Kissimmee
description: Historic district along Broadway featuring locally owned shops, restaurants, the Kissimmee Lakefront Park, and the Osceola County courthouse.

name: ChampionsGate
description: Resort and golf community at the junction of I-4 and US-27, featuring the Omni resort, vacation rentals, and proximity to Disney World.

name: Ybor City
description: Tampa's historic Latin Quarter and National Historic Landmark District featuring cigar factories, nightlife, restaurants, and the Columbia Restaurant.

name: Downtown Tampa
description: Tampa's central business district featuring Water Street Tampa, the Tampa Riverwalk, Amalie Arena, and the convention center.

name: South Tampa
description: Upscale residential area featuring Bayshore Boulevard, Hyde Park Village, SoHo district, and waterfront dining along the world's longest sidewalk.

name: Hyde Park
description: Historic Tampa neighborhood featuring upscale shopping at Hyde Park Village, trendy restaurants, and beautiful tree-lined streets near Bayshore.

name: Channelside District
description: Tampa's waterfront entertainment district near the cruise port, featuring Sparkman Wharf, the Florida Aquarium, and Water Street Tampa.

name: Water Street Tampa
description: Tampa's newest mixed-use district featuring luxury hotels, restaurants, the Tampa EDITION, and JW Marriott along the downtown waterfront.

name: Westshore
description: Tampa's business district near the airport featuring International Plaza mall, corporate offices, and major chain hotels.

name: Busch Gardens Area
description: North Tampa district centered around Busch Gardens Tampa Bay, the University of South Florida, and Adventure Island water park.

name: Clearwater Beach
description: Award-winning beach destination on the Gulf Coast barrier island, featuring sugar-white sand, Pier 60, and beachfront resorts.

name: Downtown Clearwater
description: Clearwater's mainland downtown core featuring the Coachman Park waterfront, Cleveland Street dining, and access to the beach via the Memorial Causeway.

name: Downtown St. Petersburg
description: Vibrant waterfront downtown featuring the Salvador Dali Museum, craft breweries, murals, and the St. Pete Pier.

name: St. Pete Beach
description: Award-winning Gulf Coast beach community south of St. Petersburg, home to the iconic Don CeSar hotel and TradeWinds resort.

name: Grand Central District
description: St. Petersburg's eclectic arts and antique district along Central Avenue, featuring galleries, vintage shops, and diverse restaurants.

name: Fort Lauderdale Beach
description: Iconic stretch of Atlantic coastline along A1A, featuring luxury hotels, beachside restaurants, and the famous wavewall promenade.

name: Las Olas Boulevard
description: Fort Lauderdale's premier dining and shopping boulevard connecting downtown to the beach, lined with galleries, boutiques, and restaurants.

name: Downtown Fort Lauderdale
description: Fort Lauderdale's urban core featuring the Broward Center for the Performing Arts, Museum of Discovery and Science, and the New River.

name: Port Everglades Area
description: The area surrounding Fort Lauderdale's cruise port, including nearby hotels on SE 17th Street and the Hilton Marina district.

name: Hollywood Beach
description: Charming beachfront community between Fort Lauderdale and Miami featuring the famous Hollywood Beach Broadwalk, a 2.5-mile oceanfront promenade.

name: Daytona Beach Boardwalk
description: The heart of Daytona Beach tourism featuring the oceanfront boardwalk, amusement rides, the Bandshell, and classic beach attractions.

name: Daytona International Speedway Area
description: District surrounding the World Center of Racing, buzzing during the Daytona 500, Bike Week, and Coke Zero Sugar 400 with massive bus charter demand.

name: Daytona Beach Mainland
description: The mainland side of Daytona Beach across the Halifax River, featuring LPGA Boulevard shopping, Embry-Riddle University, and residential areas.

name: Cocoa Beach Pier Area
description: The central stretch of Cocoa Beach surrounding the historic pier, featuring surf shops, restaurants, and beachfront hotels popular with pre-cruise travelers.

name: Port Canaveral Terminal Area
description: The cruise terminal district along George King Boulevard, featuring cruise parking lots, The Cove shopping area, and Exploration Tower.

name: Astronaut Boulevard Corridor
description: Cape Canaveral's main commercial strip along SR-A1A, featuring pre-cruise hotels, restaurants, and the approach to Port Canaveral.

name: Downtown Melbourne
description: Historic downtown district featuring locally owned shops and restaurants along New Haven Avenue, with regular events and farmers markets.

name: Eau Gallie Arts District
description: Melbourne's creative hub featuring art galleries, murals, studios, and restaurants along Highland Avenue in the Eau Gallie neighborhood.

name: Viera
description: Master-planned community in central Brevard County featuring The Avenue Viera shopping, USSSA Space Coast Stadium, and suburban amenities.

name: Downtown West Palm Beach
description: Vibrant downtown district featuring Clematis Street nightlife, the Kravis Center, CityPlace (Rosemary Square), and waterfront dining.
highlights: Kravis Center for the Performing Arts

name: Palm Beach Island
description: Exclusive barrier island featuring The Breakers resort, Worth Avenue luxury shopping, the Flagler Museum, and some of the most expensive real estate in the country.

name: Palm Beach Gardens
description: Upscale North Palm Beach community featuring The Gardens Mall, PGA National Resort, and miles of nature trails.

name: Downtown Lakeland
description: Historic downtown centered around Lake Mirror and Munn Park, featuring locally owned shops, restaurants, and the Polk Theatre.

name: Florida Southern College Area
description: Neighborhood surrounding Florida Southern College featuring the largest single-site collection of Frank Lloyd Wright architecture in the world.

name: Downtown Sanford
description: Historic waterfront downtown on Lake Monroe featuring antique shops, craft breweries, and the Amtrak Auto Train terminal.

name: Downtown DeLand
description: Charming Main Street America community featuring locally owned shops, restaurants, and Stetson University's campus atmosphere.

name: Siesta Key
description: Barrier island off Sarasota featuring the number-one rated beach in the United States with powdery quartz crystal sand.

name: St. Armands Circle
description: Upscale shopping and dining circle on St. Armands Key between downtown Sarasota and Lido Beach, designed by John Ringling.

name: Downtown Sarasota
description: Cultural hub of the Gulf Coast featuring the Ringling Museum, Van Wezel Performing Arts Hall, and Main Street dining and shopping.

name: Fifth Avenue South
description: Naples' premier downtown street featuring upscale dining, boutiques, galleries, and cultural venues in a walkable setting.

name: Third Street South
description: Charming shopping and dining district in Old Naples featuring boutiques, outdoor cafes, and Saturday farmers market.
